results.name: has_hook
results.detail: "A warm sauna may be *quietly good* for the heart."

results.name: has_cta
results.detail: button must be "Download Vibe Health →" + highlighted title

results.name: follows_json_schema
results.detail: key, theme not in health/looksmax, bg_prompts

rationales.hook_strength: 'Quietly good for the heart' is pleasantly understated but lacks the specificity or contrarian punch needed to stop a scroll — no number, no unexpected claim, no tension.
rationales.clarity: Each slide holds one idea and the progression (stressor → link → frequency → caution → takeaway) is logical and easy to follow.
rationales.audience_fit: Sauna access skews older and more affluent than the tired-25-year-old target; the content is relevant but the habit itself isn't realistic for most of the audience.
rationales.novelty: Sauna-heart-health is well-trodden wellness content; the honest 'association not proof' framing adds a small fresh edge but doesn't rescue a familiar angle.
rationales.brand_fit: Hedged language throughout ('linked to', 'may support', 'association not proof'), no hype words, genuine caution slide, and a CTA that ties naturally back to the app — textbook Vibe Health voice.
rationales.predicted_performance: Saves likely from the 'honest about limitations' crowd but shares will be limited because the hook doesn't provoke and the habit isn't universally accessible.
